LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT GAP, INC.'S DEMURRER TO THE ENTIRE COMPLAINT IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF DID NOT OPPOSE DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER. THE COURT RECEIVED A COPY OF PLAINTIFF'S MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS AND THAT DOCUMENT APPEARS TO BE AN OPPOSITION TO DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER AND MOTION TO STRIKE. THE MOTION FOR JUDGMENT ON THE PLEADINGS FAILS TO EXPLAIN HOW THE DEFECTIVE COMPLAINT CAN BE CURED BY AMENDMENT. SEE GOODMAN V. KENNEDY, (1976) 18 CAL.3D 335, 349. DEFENDANT'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF COMPLAINT IS OFF CALENDAR AS MOOT. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST J. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR # 5976 (302)
NOTICE OF ENTRY OF JUDGMENT OR ORDER

THE COURT ORDERED THE FOLLOWING JUDGMENT ENTERED: IT IS ADJUDGED THAT PLAINTIFF WRIGHT, FRANKLIN H TAKE NOTHING FROM DEFENDANT GAP, INC. COSTS SHALL BE AWARDED TO DEFENDANT GAP, INC. SEE SCANNED DOCUMENT

Supporting Declaration And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Pursuant To Ccp 438
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - FRANKLIN H. WRIGHT, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. CANDICE MULLINGS WILLIAMS OF PHILLIPS SPALLAS & ANGSTADT LLP, TEL. NO. 278-9400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF FRANKLIN WRIGHT's Supporting Declaration And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Pursuant To CCP 438 is Off calendar. The court received a copy of plaintiff's motion for judgment on the pleadings and that document appears to be an opposition to defendant's demurrer and motion to strike. The motion for judgment on the pleadings fails to explain how the defective complaint can be cured by amendment. See Goodman v. Kennedy, (1976) 18 Cal.3d 335, 349. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =(302/EG)

DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Aug-27-2014 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - FRANKLIN H. WRIGHT, PLAINTIFF APPEARING IN PRO PER;
ATTY. CANDICE MULLINGS WILLIAMS OF PHILLIPS SPALLAS & ANGSTADT LLP, TEL. NO. 278-9400 APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT GAP, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT - The demurrer to the entire complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff did not oppose defendant's demurrer. The court received a copy of plaintiff's motion for judgment on the pleadings and that document appears to be an opposition to defendant's demurrer and motion to strike. The motion for judgment on the pleadings fails to explain how the defective complaint can be cured by amendment. See Goodman v. Kennedy, (1976) 18 Cal.3d 335, 349. Defendant's motion to strike portions of complaint is off calendar as moot. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest J. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/eg

Supporting Declaration And Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment On The Pleadings Pursuant To Ccp 438
